From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

International sports governing body organizing field hockey

This article is about the governing body of field hockey. For the governing body of ice hockey, see [International Ice Hockey Federation](/wiki/International_Ice_Hockey_Federation "International Ice Hockey Federation").

International Hockey Federation

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| ***Fédération Internationale de Hockey*** | |
|  | |
| Abbreviation | FIH |
| Formation | 7 January 1924; 102 years ago (1924-01-07) |

The **International Hockey Federation**, commonly known by the [acronym](/wiki/Acronym_and_initialism "Acronym and initialism") **FIH**, is the international governing body of [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") and [indoor field hockey](/wiki/Indoor_field_hockey "Indoor field hockey"). Its headquarters are in [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne"), Switzerland. FIH is responsible for field hockey's major international tournaments, notably the [Hockey World Cup](/wiki/Hockey_World_Cup "Hockey World Cup").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Hockey_Federation&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

FIH was founded on 7 January 1924 in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") by Paul Léautey, who became the first president, in response to field hockey's omission from the programme of the [1924 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1924_Summer_Olympics "1924 Summer Olympics").[[4]](#cite_note-4) First members complete to join the seven founding members were Austria, [Belgium](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Hockey_Association "Royal Belgian Hockey Association"), Czechoslovakia, France, Hungary, Spain, and Switzerland.[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)

In 1982, the FIH merged with the International Federation of Women's Hockey Associations (IFWHA), which had been founded in 1927 by [Australia](/wiki/Hockey_Australia "Hockey Australia"), Denmark, [England](/wiki/Hockey_England "Hockey England"), [Ireland](/wiki/Irish_Hockey_Association "Irish Hockey Association"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scottish_Hockey_Union "Scottish Hockey Union"), South Africa, the United States, and [Wales](/wiki/Welsh_Hockey_Union "Welsh Hockey Union").[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)

The organisation has been based in [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne"), Switzerland since 2005, having moved from [Brussels](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"), Belgium.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In response to the [2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/2022_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine"), the FIH banned Russia from the [2022 Women's FIH Hockey Junior World Cup](/wiki/2022_Women%27s_FIH_Hockey_Junior_World_Cup "2022 Women's FIH Hockey Junior World Cup"), and banned Russian and Belarusian officials from FIH events.[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7)

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Hockey_Federation&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Structure")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/International_Hockey_Federation_map.png/330px-International_Hockey_Federation_map.png)](/wiki/File:International_Hockey_Federation_map.png)

Map of the World with the five confederations.

In total, there are 140 member associations within the five confederations recognised by FIH. This includes Great Britain which is recognised as an adherent member of FIH, the team was represented at the [Olympics](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the Summer Olympics") and the [Champions Trophy](/wiki/Hockey_Champions_Trophy "Hockey Champions Trophy"). England, Scotland and Wales are also represented by separate teams in FIH sanctioned tournaments.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

:   [AfHF – African Hockey Federation](/wiki/African_Hockey_Federation "African Hockey Federation")
:   [AHF – Asian Hockey Federation](/wiki/Asian_Hockey_Federation "Asian Hockey Federation")
:   [EHF – European Hockey Federation](/wiki/European_Hockey_Federation "European Hockey Federation")
:   [OHF – Oceania Hockey Federation](/wiki/Oceania_Hockey_Federation "Oceania Hockey Federation")
:   [PAHF – Pan American Hockey Federation](/wiki/Pan_American_Hockey_Federation "Pan American Hockey Federation")

The [FIH World Rankings](/wiki/FIH_World_Rankings "FIH World Rankings") was updated once after the major tournament finished, based on FIH sanction tournaments.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## FIH Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=International_Hockey_Federation&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: FIH Awards")]

The [FIH Awards](/wiki/FIH_Awards "FIH Awards") have been given annually since 1998 for men and women, while the young category was added in 2001 to honour the best performances for junior players. FIH has twelve Honorary awards given to people who have made outstanding contributions to field hockey.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
